cards.back: Next subnet starts at the address immediately after the previous subnet's broadcast (or last address). No gap.

cards.front: Next subnet starts at the address immediately after the previous subnet's broadcast (or last address). No gap.
If you carve 192.168.100.0/28 first, what's the next contiguous start?
After a /28 ending at .15, a contiguous /32 lives at?
After a /32 at .16, the next contiguous /32 lives at?
Why does /32 have no network or broadcast address?
cards.back: Mask 255.255.255.255 has zero host bits. The single address IS the network — useful only for loopbacks or host routes.

cards.front: Two usable IPs needed for a point-to-point serial link — smallest valid mask?
cards.back: /30 — block size 4, 2 usable. Smaller (/31) is RFC-3021-only, not default Cisco.
Variable Length Subnet Mask — different mask lengths within one parent network.

cards.front: Variable Length Subnet Mask — different mask lengths within one parent network.
What does `show ip route` show when a /24 is split into a /26 + /32 on the same router?
cards.back: "Variably subnetted" line followed by each child prefix with its own mask length.
